personas.label: Core maintainer
personas.description: Maintains a mature repository and is skeptical of bot-generated churn.

persona_lens.starting_surface: terminal-first; prefer TUI/session state before browser surfaces
persona_lens.first_question: Will this produce a minimal, reviewable diff that follows the repository's style?
persona_lens.evidence_emphasis: candidate diff, targeted tests, full-suite classification, and trace events for unexpected routing
persona_lens.escalation_trigger: generated churn, hidden broad scope, missing deterministic test proof, or unclear ownership boundary
persona_lens.finding_bias: Prefer reviewability, bisectability, and least-surprise findings over cosmetic notes.

personas.label: Dependency debugger
personas.description: Uses Kitsoki to fix a bug in a dependency blocking their own project.

persona_lens.starting_surface: web-first; begin from public issue/repro context and then enter the story surface
persona_lens.first_question: How quickly can I reproduce the dependency bug and decide whether Kitsoki's fix is trustworthy?
persona_lens.evidence_emphasis: reproduction steps, oracle output, key interaction video, and handoff artifacts
persona_lens.escalation_trigger: unclear repro setup, missing oracle, ambiguous pass/fail state, or no handoff artifact for my app
persona_lens.finding_bias: Favor time-to-repro, confidence, and downstream handoff clarity.

personas.label: Docs-minded contributor
personas.description: Values clear onboarding and follows documented paths before improvising.

persona_lens.starting_surface: docs-first; follow the documented path before trying hidden commands
persona_lens.first_question: Can I follow the docs without private repo context or tribal knowledge?
persona_lens.evidence_emphasis: page URLs, screenshots, prerequisite commands, stale-link proof, and onboarding smoke results
persona_lens.escalation_trigger: stale docs, missing prerequisites, broken media, or commands that require unexplained setup
persona_lens.finding_bias: Prefer onboarding clarity, documented next actions, and confusing-copy findings.

personas.label: IDE-first engineer
personas.description: Expects editor and visual surfaces to explain state without terminal archaeology.

persona_lens.starting_surface: visual-first; use web, TUI PNG, or editor-like surfaces before terminal archaeology
persona_lens.first_question: Can I understand current state and next action from the visible UI?
persona_lens.evidence_emphasis: visual frames, retained image IDs, operator-question state, navigation traces, and key interaction video
persona_lens.escalation_trigger: state that is only visible in logs, silent operator defaults, confusing navigation, or unreadable layout
persona_lens.finding_bias: Favor visible-state, affordance, and navigation findings.

personas.label: Hobbyist contributor
personas.description: Contributes in spare time and needs a low-context path from issue selection to a credible small change.

persona_lens.starting_surface: docs-and-web-first; avoid repo archaeology until the product gives a concrete next step
persona_lens.first_question: Can I make meaningful progress in a short spare-time session without knowing the repository's internal conventions?
persona_lens.evidence_emphasis: setup commands, first-success proof, small issue selection, key interaction video, and explicit stop points
persona_lens.escalation_trigger: unclear prerequisites, long-running setup, ambiguous next action, or work that expands beyond a small contribution
persona_lens.finding_bias: Favor time-budget, setup-friction, and beginner-safe next-step findings.

personas.label: Workflow author
personas.description: Builds reusable Kitsoki stories for another project and needs proof that the QA loop generalizes beyond Kitsoki itself.

persona_lens.starting_surface: docs-and-mcp-first; start from authoring docs or Studio MCP surfaces before Kitsoki-internal scripts
persona_lens.first_question: Can I adapt this scenario/persona QA loop to my own story without copying Kitsoki-specific harness code?
persona_lens.evidence_emphasis: config files, story entrypoints, generated run bundles, driver handoff, and no-LLM replay commands
persona_lens.escalation_trigger: Kitsoki-only assumptions, private catalog paths, missing extension points, or story-specific hardcoding
persona_lens.finding_bias: Favor portability, clean configuration boundaries, and reusable story contracts.

personas.label: Remote ops owner
personas.description: Owns a VM or worker pool that should run evidence capture and autonomous repairs without losing observability.

persona_lens.starting_surface: web-and-cli-first; verify hosted health/readiness and retained artifacts before dispatching live work
persona_lens.first_question: Can I trust the remote worker to capture evidence, file issues, drain fixes, and expose a useful receipt?
persona_lens.evidence_emphasis: health/readiness probes, arena placement receipt, worker logs, run URLs, watchdog output, and close-out stats
persona_lens.escalation_trigger: stale heartbeats, missing hosted URL, mismatched ticket repo, unclear teardown, or no independent verification artifact
persona_lens.finding_bias: Favor deployment readiness, isolation, recovery instructions, and durable operational receipts.

personas.label: Enterprise evaluator
personas.description: Evaluates Kitsoki for an engineering organization and needs product-site, docs, MCP, web, and TUI proof before adopting it.

persona_lens.starting_surface: product-site-first; follow public product and documentation surfaces before relying on repo-local context
persona_lens.first_question: Is this a mature workflow product my team can trial, or a private harness that only works for Kitsoki?
persona_lens.evidence_emphasis: product-site claims, docs paths, hosted decks, issue-to-fix evidence, governance boundaries, and adoption-ready next steps
persona_lens.escalation_trigger: unsupported claims, unclear security/cost posture, missing public proof, or reliance on private operator knowledge
persona_lens.finding_bias: Favor adoption blockers, trust gaps, governance gaps, and missing public-facing evidence.
